import org.slf4j.Logger;

// Same-branch doctrine: a guard's throw covers only its own leg. Every other
// path must report, print, throw, or carry its own `// no-report:` - a throw
// elsewhere in the catch no longer sanctifies a silent leg.
class Jv001GuardSwallow {
    private final Logger log;
    private final boolean fatal;

    Jv001GuardSwallow(Logger log, boolean fatal) {
        this.log = log;
        this.fatal = fatal;
    }

    void silentFallThrough() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV001.*ends at line 23"
            if (fatal) {
                throw new RuntimeException(e);
            }
            cleanup();
        }
    }

    void reportedFallThrough() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            if (fatal) {
                throw new RuntimeException(e);
            }
            log.error("recovered", e);
            cleanup();
        }
    }

    // A statement marker covers exactly the paths that execute the marked
    // statement - here the fall-through leg.
    void markedFallThrough() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            if (fatal) {
                throw new RuntimeException(e);
            }
            // no-report: best-effort cleanup; a failure here is not actionable
            cleanup();
        }
    }

    void silentEarlyReturn() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV001.*ends at line 57"
            if (!fatal) {
                return;
            }
            log.error("failed", e);
        }
    }

    void silentElseLeg() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV001.*ends at line 72"
            if (fatal) {
                throw new RuntimeException(e);
            } else {
                cleanup();
            }
        }
    }

    void cleanup() {}

    void risky() throws Exception {}
}
